ab: Nga’ay ho, tahidangen no singsi kamo a miliso to malitengay no niyaro’ anini.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: Hai, sano ‘Amisen niyam a somowal ko malitengay.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說阿美語。

ab: Tatahila to kita haw?
en: A: Are we almost there?

ab: Tatahila to.
en: B:Almost there.

ab: Fofo, tayniay to kami a miliso kisonan.
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: Mafana’ay kamo a caciyaw to no ‘Amis ha! tada fangcal!
ch: B:你們會說阿美語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B: You speak Pangcah! That’s great!

ab: Nga’ay ho, tahidangen no singsi kamo tayra i omah no lamelo a maomah.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

ab: Hai, singsi.
en: B: Yes, sir.

ab: Tayni a midado’do’ mapolong, alaen ko sakimaomah.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: Hai singsi, nialaan to niyam ko sakimaomah.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.

ab: Tahiniay to kita haw?
en: A: Are we there yet?

ab: Cowa ho, malaayay ho.
en: B: Not yet, it’s still far away.

ab: Oli! micefos to nanom kiso.
en: A: You go watering.

ab: Hayda, micefos to nanom kako.
en: B: Okay, I’ll water it.

ab: Alaen ko sapilaplap to ayam a tekap kiso.
en: A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: Hai, milaplap kako to ayam.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: Ngaay ho, tatayla kita i loma’ no aadopen anini.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: Aciyah ka fangcal! o maan ko sakatayra ita?
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?

ab: O paso ko sakatayra ita.
en: A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: Hongilaen ko pido’do’, miaca kita to paya.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

ab: Hai.
en: B: Yes.

ab: O ayam ko i kaayaway , o oner ko I kaikolay, niyah sa a miaraw.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

ab: Singsi mataraw kako to oner.
en: B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: Ngaay to, miaraw kiso to ayam.
en: A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ab: Cecay a tatokian minokay a masaopo.
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.

ab: Hai, akowiday singsi.
en: B: Yes, thank you.

ab: O manan ko manengnengay no miso anini?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: Adihayay ko oner, ira ho ko ayam ko manengnengay no mako.
ch: B:看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.

ab: Halikien, wa malingad to kita.
en: A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: Talaen ho kako, marorayay to ko o’o no mako.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: malacafay kita a midangoy haw?
en: A: Shall we go swimming together?

ab: Hayda! Ata!
en: B:Yes! Let’s go!

ab: Aloman ko tamdaw midangoyay itini.
en: A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: Angongolay ko nanom itira, itini sa kita a midangoy.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
